page.home.title: SHINYLOGIC — Forge equipment data into decisions.
page.home.description: SHINYLOGIC delivers the full IT/OT+AI stack directly to high-volume fabs: six-layer architecture, defined SLAs, 100% archived cross-fab templates. FAB300 reference build powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72.
home.hero.lead: SHINYLOGIC delivers the complete IT/OT+AI technology stack directly to your fab — from L1 equipment signals to L6 resilience layer. We assume full accountability for architecture design, integration, and operations under defined SLAs, and hand back 100% archived cross-fab templates.
home.section01.prose1: SHINYLOGIC is not an equipment vendor — we are your fab's IT/OT+AI full-stack systems integrator. We assume direct, end-to-end accountability for architecture design, hardware and software selection, on-site implementation, and ongoing operations, backed by defined SLAs. Our competitive benchmark is not other integrators — it is your in-house build option.
home.section01.trustLine: Every SLO commitment is backed by an industrial-grade resilient architecture — not an expectation-management document. Verified through real drills, not theoretical projections.
home.section06.ecosystem: Powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 as the AI compute foundation: 3 × GB300 NVL72 (liquid-cooled) · 4 × HGX B300 inference · Quantum-X800 800Gb/s XDR · Spectrum-X SN5600 · NVMe data lake.
engage.title: Delivering the IT/OT+AI Full Stack for Your Fab
engage.body: Your fab focuses on process excellence; we assume direct accountability for building, integrating, and operating the IT/OT+AI full stack under defined SLAs — and hand back 100% archived cross-fab templates so your knowledge assets always remain yours.
page.methodology.title: Methodology · FAB300 — SHINYLOGIC Smart Fab Delivery Methodology
page.methodology.description: FAB300 reference methodology: six-layer × four-phase delivery matrix, 8 Gate checkpoints, DR RTO ≤ 4hr, system SLO ≥ 95%, 100% cross-fab archiving. SHINYLOGIC delivers a replicable IT/OT+AI full-stack integration methodology for fabs.
methodology.hero.lede: A replicable delivery system centered on the high-volume MES platform (FAB300) with GB300 NVL72 AI Fabric as compute foundation, covering the full six-layer architecture end to end — four phases, 8 Gates, defined SLAs, 100% archived.
methodology.hero.stat1.label: Full-Stack Architecture Coverage
methodology.hero.stat1.sub: Input · Data · Compute · Application · Governance · Resilience
methodology.hero.stat4.label: System SLO
methodology.section02.lede: Complete matrix of 10 delivery categories across phases P1–P4 — each cell is a contractually traceable delivery milestone. Aligned to the six-layer architecture · delivery scope confirmed per contract.
methodology.section03.lede: P1 Facility → P2 Installation → P3 Pilot → P4 Production, spanning M1 through M22+, with 8 Gate checkpoints at each transition. Operations commence from the installation phase; DR first-run drill completed during the pilot phase.
methodology.section03.p1.body: Facility construction, liquid-cooled data center, power systems, basic network infrastructure; Gate 1–2 environment acceptance
methodology.section03.p2.body: GB300 NVL72 primary delivery and rack installation, AI Fabric build-out, high-volume MES platform go-live, OT security integration; Gate 3–5
methodology.section03.p3.body: System integration testing, AI model training, DR first-run drill, yield ramp acceptance; Gate 6–7
methodology.section03.p4.body: Full-rate production, SOC 24×7 takeover, SLO ≥ 95% continuous monitoring, DR quarterly drill cadence, cross-fab template 100% archived; Gate 8
methodology.section04.lede: Five KPI commitments written into the contract, enforced by 8 Gate acceptance checkpoints.
methodology.section04.kpi1.label: Yield Target ≥ 90%
methodology.section04.kpi1.body: AI-assisted process control, real-time SPC/YMS feedback, active from the production ramp stage.
methodology.section04.kpi2.label: System SLO ≥ 95%
methodology.section04.kpi2.body: Full-stack six-layer system availability commitment, guaranteed by SOC 24×7 monitoring, covering MES and AI Fabric.
methodology.section04.kpi3.label: DR RTO ≤ 4hr
methodology.section04.kpi3.body: Recovery time objective for critical systems; warm standby architecture + SD-WAN dual-ISP redundancy, verified quarterly.
methodology.section04.kpi4.label: DR RPO ≤ 15min
methodology.section04.kpi4.body: Recovery point objective; asynchronous replication ensures production data loss does not exceed 15 minutes.
methodology.section04.kpi5.label: Cross-Fab Template Archive 100%
methodology.section04.kpi5.body: Upon production phase completion, the full set of build configurations, processes, and AI models are archived as a cross-fab replication template.
methodology.section04.governanceNote: 8 Gates aligned to four-phase build · RACI single Accountable owner (A) per item · Three-tier risk (Red/Yellow/Green) escalation · DR drills embedded in quarterly Review cycle · Cross-fab template 100% archived
methodology.section05.lede: Six risk domains, each with a dedicated mitigation — from supply chain to workforce, fully transparent. Three-tier escalation (Red/Yellow/Green): Yellow escalates to project committee within 48 hours; Red is reported immediately to the client's decision-maker.
methodology.section06.lede: After the first fab is delivered, knowledge assets are handed over as 100% archived templates — enabling the second fab to start faster and reach pilot production with greater precision.
methodology.section06.step1.body: Upon production phase completion, the full set of build configurations, process documents, and AI models are archived to form the cross-fab replication baseline.
methodology.section06.step2.body: Assess differences in equipment vendors, process nodes, facility conditions, and regulatory environment for the new fab to confirm the scope of customization.
methodology.section06.step3.body: Tailor the template per the gap assessment results and execute the new fab build; installation phase projected to shorten by 20–30%, pilot integration by 15–25%.
methodology.section06.templateItem1: Six-layer architecture design documents and BOM
methodology.section06.templateItem5: DR drill scripts and acceptance criteria
methodology.section06.disclaimerText: Templates reduce replication cost but do not eliminate customization work. Each fab differs in equipment vendors, process nodes, and facility conditions — a gap assessment is required before confirming scope and schedule. The second fab is projected to achieve a 20–30% reduction in installation phase duration and a 15–25% reduction in pilot integration cycle time (subject to gap complexity).
page.contact.title: Book a Consultation — SHINYLOGIC
page.contact.description: Start a planning conversation with SHINYLOGIC: clarify IT/OT+AI full-stack requirements, L1–L6 delivery scope, SLA metrics, and Gate milestones. Service location: Hsinchu, Taiwan — response within two business days.
contact.hero.lede: Start a conversation with us to clarify build requirements and full-stack delivery scope. Service location: Taiwan · Hsinchu · N 24°08′ E 120°41′.
contact.section00.processNote: Our competitive benchmark is your in-house build team — we offer the alternative of outsourcing to a specialist full-stack team, from first conversation to 100% archiving, fully transparent with no gray areas.
contact.direct.email.sub: Send inquiries, media, or recruitment questions — all received and categorized through the same inbox.
contact.direct.response.sub: We will prioritize identifying the inquiry type upon receipt; if additional information is needed, it will be noted in the first reply.
contact.direct.langs.sub: Conversations in your preferred language; technical specification documents provided in all three languages.
contact.section03.intro: Select the inquiry entry point that best fits your fab's current situation. Each type maps to a different technical depth and business path; feel free to select multiple — our first conversation will further clarify scope.

page.solutions.title: Solutions — SHINYLOGIC
page.solutions.description: SHINYLOGIC six-layer full-stack solutions: AI compute and storage, manufacturing execution MES, high-speed network fabric, IT/OT security, off-site DR, enterprise integration — each layer addressing one problem domain, delivered under defined SLAs.
solutions.hero.eyebrow: Solutions · Six-Layer Full Stack // SOLUTIONS OVERVIEW
solutions.hero.zhTitle: Each layer solves one class of problem.
solutions.hero.enLabel: SIX LAYERS · SIX SOLUTION DOMAINS · ONE CONTRACTUAL SLA
solutions.hero.lede: SHINYLOGIC does not deliver modules — we deliver full-stack accountability spanning L1 to L6. From equipment signals to resilience architecture, every layer has a defined problem statement, delivery scope, and verifiable acceptance criteria.
solutions.section02.lead: The six-layer architecture is not a diagram — it is six independently verifiable engineering problem domains. Each layer maps to a real challenge: heterogeneous protocol silos (L1), high-frequency data unable to support AI (L2), supercompute deployment (L3), decision loops that cannot scale (L4), blurred OT/IT compliance boundaries (L5), and prohibitively high fab downtime costs (L6).
solutions.section03.lead: Click any solution to expand delivery scope and key specifications. All figures are contractual terms — verifiable, not estimated.
solutions.section04.lead: Six KPI commitments derived from the same contractual SLA — not targets, but auditable delivery conditions.

page.careers.title: Careers — IT Intelligence Division · SHINYLOGIC
page.careers.description: Join SHINYLOGIC's IT Intelligence Division — eight functional teams, four-phase build, six-layer architecture in real production environments. Open roles: IT/OT Architect, MES Engineer, AI/MLOps, OT Security, Network Engineer, DR Engineer.
careers.hero.eyebrow: Careers · IT Intelligence Division // JOIN THE BUILD
careers.hero.zhTitle: Join SHINYLOGIC to Build the AI Intelligence Layer for Fabs
careers.hero.enLabel: IT INTELLIGENCE DIVISION — OPEN FOR TALENT
careers.hero.lede: We are building the IT/OT+AI full stack for a real fab in production — GB300 NVL72, FAB300 MES, Digital Twin, SOC 24×7. This is not a sandbox environment; it is a real production site with eight functional teams, four build phases, and a six-layer architecture.

careers.tech.T2.desc: SECS-GEM / GEM300 equipment integration, WIP dispatch optimization, APC R2R+FDC closed-loop — full system integration under real fab production load, not a demo environment.
careers.tech.T3.desc: An NVIDIA Omniverse-based Digital Twin combined with Agentic RAG, forging equipment sensor data from the NVMe Lakehouse all the way into actionable AI decisions.
careers.tech.T4.desc: OT intrusion detection, asset visibility, SIEM/SOAR round-the-clock operations, NGFW/EDR/IAM/PAM — aligned to IEC 62443 and 等保 2.0 三級, defense-in-depth from L1 equipment to L5 governance layer.

careers.principle.01.body: Every technical decision has one and only one Accountable Owner (A). Contract Gates confirm readiness to proceed — engineering judgment is never replaced by hierarchical approval.
careers.principle.02.body: SLO ≥ 95%, RTO ≤ 4hr, yield ≥ 90% written into the contract — not expectation documents, but verifiable delivery standards backed by real drills.
careers.principle.03.body: Three-tier risk escalation (Red/Yellow/Green): Yellow escalates to project committee within 48 hours; DR quarterly drill results are archived; cross-fab templates 100% archived, no knowledge silos.

page.about.title: About SHINYLOGIC — Mission, Governance & Delivery Capability
page.about.description: SHINYLOGIC assumes direct end-to-end accountability for IT/OT+AI full-stack build, integration, and operations for high-volume fabs. Six-layer architecture, eight Gate governance, quarterly DR drills, 100% knowledge archive handover. FAB300 reference build, NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 compute foundation.
about.hero.lede: SHINYLOGIC assumes direct end-to-end accountability for IT/OT+AI full-stack build, integration, and operations for high-volume fabs — with defined SLAs guaranteeing delivery quality, FAB300 as the reference build, and NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 as the compute foundation.
about.hero.stat0.label: DR RTO
about.hero.stat1.label: System SLO
about.hero.stat2.label: Knowledge Archive
about.hero.stat3.label: Gates
about.section01.prose1: SHINYLOGIC is not an equipment vendor — we are your fab's IT/OT+AI full-stack systems integrator. We assume direct end-to-end accountability for high-volume fabs, from architecture design and hardware/software selection to on-site implementation and ongoing operations, backed by defined SLAs rather than expectation management.
about.section01.prose2: FAB300 is our reference build: with NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 as the compute foundation, it integrates the six-layer architecture from L1 equipment signals to L6 cross-region DR, covering the full IT/OT+AI stack for high-volume fabs. It also incorporates HGX B300 inference nodes and Quantum-X800 800Gb/s XDR high-speed Fabric to ensure the compute ecosystem meets Blackwell Ultra generation engineering standards.
about.section01.trustLine: Our competitive benchmark is not other integrators — it is your in-house build option. Every commitment we take on is backed by SLA and Gate acceptance documentation, verified through real drills, not theoretical projections.
about.gov.raci.title: RACI Single Accountable Owner
about.gov.raci.body: A single Accountable Owner (A) is designated for every item, eliminating the blind spot of "multiple owners = nobody owns it." The RACI matrix is updated at each delivery phase, ensuring every decision has a clear owner — and the client can query accountability at any time.
about.gov.gate.title: 8-Gate Checkpoint
about.gov.gate.body: Acceptance documents are submitted at each gate; the next phase only begins after confirmation. Gate 1–2: data center civil works and environment acceptance; Gate 3–5: GB300 NVL72 primary delivery and MES go-live; Gate 6–7: high-volume integration and DR first-run drill; Gate 8: full-rate production go-live. No skipping gates, no verbal confirmation.
about.gov.risk.title: Three-Tier Risk Escalation
about.gov.risk.body: Green: routine tracking, updated at next weekly meeting. Yellow: mandatory escalation to project committee within 48 hours, countermeasures activated. Red: immediate escalation to the client's decision-maker, no buffer, no waiting. All escalation records are archived and included in the next Gate acceptance review.
about.gov.dr.title: Quarterly DR Drill
about.gov.dr.body: RTO ≤ 4hr is not a paper commitment: a full DR drill is conducted every quarter, simulating cross-region failure scenarios and recording actual recovery times. Results are archived and reviewed in the following quarter, continuously verifying resilience capability and ensuring commitments match reality.
about.trust.methodology.title: Methodology, Documentation, Drills
about.trust.methodology.body: SHINYLOGIC's delivery methodology is anchored by 8 Gates, each with complete acceptance documentation on file to eliminate verbal commitments. DR drill results are archived and continuously improved, ensuring written SLAs remain aligned with on-site resilience capability over time.
about.trust.nolockIn.title: Knowledge Returns to You
about.trust.nolockIn.body: Upon delivery completion, all architecture decision documents, configuration templates, operating SOPs, and DR drill records are 100% handed over to the client. Cross-fab replication does not depend on SHINYLOGIC — your knowledge assets always remain in your hands, and the second fab can directly apply the same templates.
about.trust.baseline.title: Competing Against Your In-House Build Cost
about.trust.baseline.body: SHINYLOGIC pricing is benchmarked directly against in-house talent costs, recruitment timelines, and technical risk. OT/IT full-stack talent for semiconductor fabs is scarce; building in-house capability typically takes more than two production cycles, and accumulated knowledge is difficult to retain.
about.trust.ecosystem.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra Ecosystem Foundation
about.trust.ecosystem.body: The FAB300 reference build adopts NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 (liquid-cooled) and HGX B300 inference nodes as compute core, paired with Quantum-X800 800Gb/s XDR Fabric and NVMe Lakehouse — no transitional solutions, ensuring the compute ecosystem meets GB300 generation engineering standards.

buildvsbuy.heading: Why Outsource Beats Building In-House
buildvsbuy.lead: Competitive benchmark: the fab's in-house IT team. The following three structural gaps explain why SHINYLOGIC is the faster, lower-risk choice.
buildvsbuy.talent.title: Talent Scarcity
buildvsbuy.talent.body: Semiconductor data engineers are globally scarce. In-house teams average 9–14 months to recruit engineers with EDA domain knowledge — during which systems go unmaintained and yield analysis stalls. Our dedicated team is already in place and ready to engage.
buildvsbuy.schedule.title: Schedule Risk
buildvsbuy.schedule.body: On-site teams face inevitable schedule slippage from staff turnover, holidays, or urgent interruptions. Our off-site resilience architecture and on-call SLA guarantee recovery from any single point of failure within 4 hours — delivery is never hostage to a single person's availability.
buildvsbuy.archive.title: No Lock-In · Full Archive
buildvsbuy.archive.metricLabel: Full data handover upon contract close
buildvsbuy.archive.body: All code, process documentation, and historical data are committed to client-controlled repositories in open formats. The contract may be terminated at any point — no migration fees, no proprietary format lock-in. Your data is always yours.

scan.figcaption.about: Sub-fab underside view: utility conduits, facility coverage layers, and structural shell — illustrating governance depth
scan.figcaption.solutions: Process tool line close-up: wafer flow path leads from foreground through six solution domain clusters

diagram.tool.figcaption: Generic semiconductor process tool exploded-assembly schematic (not a depiction of any specific vendor's equipment): pedestal, chamber body, electrostatic chuck + wafer (throughput path, gold), gas showerhead, chamber lid, EFEM atmospheric transfer, load port, and FOUP pod — illustrating full six-layer stack control coverage.
diagram.tool.disclaimer: Schematic · generic illustration · not a depiction of any specific vendor's equipment
diagram.fab.disclaimer: Schematic section · generic facility architecture · not any specific facility
diagram.fab.figcaption: Cleanroom facility-airflow cross-section schematic (generic architecture, not any specific facility): top-to-bottom — pressurized ceiling plenum, FFU fan-filter bank, HEPA/ULPA terminal filter face, ISO Class 5 process bay (with a half-sectioned process tool), perforated raised access floor, sub-fab return-air plenum with an N+1 air-handling unit, and a right-side return-air chase closing the laminar loop; sage marks full-bay laminar coverage, clay marks N+1 air-handling redundancy.
diagram.rack.disclaimer: Schematic · generic MGX-class rack · not a depiction of any specific vendor's equipment
diagram.rack.figcaption: Compute-rack L1-L6 vertical exploded schematic (schematic, generic MGX-class rack, not a depiction of any specific vendor's equipment): bottom-to-top — rack base (PDU + liquid cooling, L1), data/storage tray (L2), GPU compute tray (L3, gold, throughput core), NVLink network spine (L3), MES control module (L4), OT/IT security gateway (L5, sage), resilience / DR module (L6, clay), and top cap; a gold dataflow climbs the full stack.
diagram.gate.disclaimer: Schematic process diagram · P&ID-style generic · original drawing
diagram.gate.figcaption: Four-phase, eight-gate delivery process schematic (P&ID-style orthogonal schematic, original generic): a main process line runs left-to-right linking P1 Plan/Blueprint, P2 Build/Integration, P3 Commission/Validation, P4 Production/Optimization; eight gate decision nodes G1-G8 sit inline as instrument bubbles (sage = governance), with valves, a pump, sensors and interlock symbols; gold marks the P4 yield/SLO sensor, clay marks the rollback/resilience bypass.
diagram.station.disclaimer: Schematic plan · generic duty-station layout · original drawing
diagram.station.figcaption: Duty-station plan layout with a small section (top-down plan + section inset, original generic): a central handover console is the team coordination & growth hub (gold), ringed by eight function workstations T1-T8 (Architecture & Governance, MES, AI/MLOps, Network Fabric, Security/SOC, DR Continuity, Enterprise Integration, Central Command CCC); walls are section poche; sage marks the SOC video-wall sightline coverage, clay marks the DR on-call resilience station; includes a north arrow, scale bar, an A-A section line and a section elevation inset at lower-right, with a star of links showing team topology.
diagram.netzone.disclaimer: Schematic topology · based on public zone/DMZ principles · original drawing
diagram.netzone.figcaption: OT/IT security network-topology schematic (orthogonal topology plan, based on public zone/DMZ principles, original drawing): a left OT zone (PLC, SCADA/HMI, OT historian collector, OT firewall) connects through a central DMZ buffer (unidirectional data diode, reverse proxy/jump host, message broker, DMZ firewall) to a right IT zone (Lakehouse/EDA, AI/RAG platform, identity & access); gold is the primary data link (one-way uplink), clay is the DR replication link, sage is full-domain SOC monitoring coverage; dotted lines are trust boundaries and the DMZ is shown with section poche.

experience.ctaLine: Ready to go deeper? Explore the full IT/OT+AI solution stack — six layers, defined SLAs, industrial-grade delivery.
